mechanism_narrative: A sustained low level of the federal funds rate indicates an accommodative monetary policy stance, which reduces the cost of capital and supports higher equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:low interest rates support asset prices
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with low FEDFUNDS levels over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals a deteriorating labor market and broader economic contraction, leading to reduced corporate earnings expectations and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points from its 3-month low
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive or flat in more than 50% of instances where the UNRATE trigger is met.

mechanism_narrative: Weak retail sales growth combined with elevated interest rates indicates a severe consumer spending slowdown exacerbated by tight monetary conditions, which significantly dampens corporate revenue growth and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month annualized growth falls below 0% AND FEDFUNDS is above 3%
evidence_refs: fact:consumer spending is a major GDP component
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are positive when both retail sales growth is negative and FEDFUNDS is above 3%, or the interaction term lacks statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp and sustained decline in housing starts during an otherwise expanding economy signals a significant weakening in the construction sector and related industries, indicating broader economic fragility and leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month moving average falls below its 12-month average by more than 10%
evidence_refs: fact:housing is a cyclical economic indicator
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns remain positive or flat during periods of significant housing starts decline within an expansionary regime.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained and robust growth in industrial production indicates strong manufacturing activity and overall economic health, which translates into higher corporate profits and supports positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are negative or flat when industrial production growth is strong and sustained.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent positive growth in non-farm payrolls signifies a healthy and expanding labor market, boosting consumer confidence and spending, which in turn drives corporate revenues and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 1-month change is positive for 3 consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are negative or flat despite sustained positive payroll growth over multiple periods.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes corporate profit margins and consumer purchasing power, while also prompting tighter monetary policy, collectively leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:high inflation can lead to rate hikes
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are consistently positive or flat during periods of high and accelerating inflation.

mechanism_narrative: A significant deceleration or contraction in real GDP growth indicates a broad economic slowdown or recession, which directly impacts corporate earnings and investor sentiment, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth falls below 1%
evidence_refs: fact:GDP is a primary measure of economic health
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive or flat when real GDP growth is significantly decelerating or contracting.
